title: Getting started with BamScale
text: BamScale provides multithreaded sequential BAM traversal R/Bioconductor workflows. package designed users already rely Rsamtools, GenomicAlignments, BiocParallel, need faster BAM parsing without moving separate non-Bioconductor workflow model. package targets three common classes BAM access: metadata-oriented scans filtering, fragment summaries, quality control generation alignment objects downstream GenomicAlignments workflows sequence quality extraction, including optimized compact mode motivation inclusion Bioconductor therefore straightforward: BamScale accelerates core data-access bottleneck preserving familiar Bioconductor inputs, filtering semantics, output types.

title: Getting started with BamScale
text: present, BamScale depends ompBAM engine intended use OpenMP-capable toolchain.

title: Getting started with BamScale
Getting started with BamScale
text: common BAM-access pattern extraction alignment metadata read name, flag, reference name, position, mapping quality, CIGAR string.

title: Getting started with BamScale
text: BamScale accepts Rsamtools::ScanBamParam, allowing existing filtering logic reused directly.
code: param <- Rsamtools::ScanBamParam(     what = c("qname", "flag", "mapq"),     mapqFilter = 20L,     flag = Rsamtools::scanBamFlag(isUnmappedQuery = FALSE) )  filtered <- bam_read(     file = bam,     param = param,     as = "data.frame",     threads = 2 )  head(filtered) #>                                       qname flag mapq #> 1  ST-E00600:137:H77Y3CCXY:1:1101:6837:1309  163  255 #> 2  ST-E00600:137:H77Y3CCXY:1:1101:6837:1309   83  255 #> 3 ST-E00600:137:H77Y3CCXY:1:1101:10450:1309  163  255 #> 4 ST-E00600:137:H77Y3CCXY:1:1101:10450:1309   83  255 #> 5 ST-E00600:137:H77Y3CCXY:1:1101:15077:1309   99  255 #> 6 ST-E00600:137:H77Y3CCXY:1:1101:15077:1309  147  255

title: Getting started with BamScale
text: downstream workflows expect GenomicAlignments objects, bam_read() can return directly.

title: Getting started with BamScale
text: bam_count() provides chromosome-level count summaries using BAM filtering model.

title: Getting started with BamScale
text: BamScale intended complement, replace, existing Bioconductor packages. Rsamtools remains canonical low-level BAM access layer R defines filtering idioms BamScale reuses ScanBamParam. GenomicAlignments remains standard package alignment-centric downstream workflows, BamScale supports direct generation compatible alignment objects. BiocParallel remains standard mechanism file-level parallelism, BamScale adds second axis parallelism per-file OpenMP threads. main difference therefore performance-oriented: BamScale accelerates traversal step staying close existing Bioconductor usage patterns.

title: Benchmarking BamScale Across Step1, GAlignments, and SeqQual Workloads
text: article combines final benchmark runs used current BamScale benchmark summary: step1 galignments run_20260320_133141 seqqual run_20260320_162359 runs generated server-first benchmark harness, balanced profile family, deterministic case order, worker/thread budget policy. Seqqual reported separately includes fair compatibility track optimized compact track.

title: Benchmarking BamScale Across Step1, GAlignments, and SeqQual Workloads
text: Step1 GAlignments run: run_20260320_133141 SeqQual run: run_20260320_162359 Step1/GAlignments results directory: /home/runner/work/BamScale/BamScale/inst/benchmarks/run_20260320_133141 SeqQual results directory: /home/runner/work/BamScale/BamScale/inst/benchmarks/run_20260320_162359

title: Benchmarking BamScale Across Step1, GAlignments, and SeqQual Workloads
text: benchmark suite covers three distinct access patterns: step1: alignment-metadata extraction without sequence/quality materialization. good proxy BAM filtering, fragment-length profiling, fragment-distribution QC. galignments: construction alignment objects suitable Bioconductor workflows centered GenomicAlignments. fair compatibility mode, closest comparator Rsamtools::scanBam optimized compact mode, reduces internal overhead interpreted optimized BamScale track rather strict apples--apples replacement compatibility path Across runs, benchmark design emphasizes: deterministic case order balanced BamScale worker/thread plans explicit comparator baselines single-file multi-file reporting fixed 48-thread budget multi-file plans

title: Benchmarking BamScale Across Step1, GAlignments, and SeqQual Workloads
text: underlying four selected BAMs used runs, repeated files allowed populate 12-file multi-file benchmark set.

title: Benchmarking BamScale Across Step1, GAlignments, and SeqQual Workloads
text: benchmark shows consistent pattern across final runs: step1 galignments strongest BamScale workloads. workloads, best BamScale plans usually low-worker, high-thread configurations, indicating within-file multithreading contributes aggressively increasing number file workers. Seqqual difficult sequence quality extraction amplifies output-construction overhead. BamScale compact mode narrows gap substantially produces strongest single-file seqqual result, best multi-file seqqual comparator remains faster. practical terms, results support following guidance: use BamScale metadata extraction alignment-object construction bottleneck BAM-heavy Bioconductor workflows favor low-worker, high-thread plans traversing one small number large BAM files interpret compact seqqual optimized throughput path rather strict compatibility benchmark

title: Bioconductor-Friendly Multithreaded BAM Processing
text: Bioconductor-Friendly Multithreaded BAM Processing  BamScale multithreaded BAM processing package R built top ompBAM C++ engine. designed Bioconductor users need high-throughput BAM parsing preserving familiar Rsamtools GenomicAlignments workflow patterns.

title: Bioconductor-Friendly Multithreaded BAM Processing
text: BamScale focuses three goals: speed modern multi-core systems, compatibility common Bioconductor input/output contracts, transparent benchmarking reproducibility. many Bioconductor pipelines, BAM access still major bottleneck. core bottleneck BAM decompression , also fact existing R-facing workflows often rely effectively single-threaded per-file access patterns. BamScale addresses exposing OpenMP threading ompBAM engine directly R interface, still returning Bioconductor-friendly objects. practical design goal replace familiar Bioconductor workflows separate ecosystem. Instead, BamScale aims preserve way users already work : Rsamtools::scanBam()-style field extraction, GenomicAlignments::readGAlignments()-style alignment-object workflows, BiocParallel file-level execution multiple BAMs processed together. means BamScale intended fit existing tooling already fits, additional within-file threading axis can remove current parsing bottleneck alignment-centric workloads. Key capabilities: OpenMP-enabled per-file parallelism via threads, optional multi-file parallelism via BiocParallel (BPPARAM), ScanBamParam-like filtering (mapqFilter, flag, , , tag), data.frame S4Vectors::DataFrame, GenomicAlignments::GAlignments, GenomicAlignments::GAlignmentPairs, scanBam-shaped list output (= "scanBam"). , step1 refers common alignment-metadata extraction workload built fields : qname flag rname pos mapq cigar kind BAM access pattern used many downstream QC, filtering, fragment-level summary workflows.

title: Bioconductor-Friendly Multithreaded BAM Processing
text: BamScale designed familiar users already work Rsamtools GenomicAlignments. step1-style metadata extraction maps naturally onto scanBam()-like use cases. = "GAlignments" = "GAlignmentPairs" support alignment-object workflows used downstream Bioconductor. BiocParallel integration preserves standard file-level parallel workflow model. main added capability BamScale can also use multiple threads within BAM file ompBAM. key difference baseline workflow pattern main reason can remove read-parsing bottlenecks workloads metadata extraction alignment-object construction.

title: Bioconductor-Friendly Multithreaded BAM Processing
text: R C++17 toolchain OpenMP-capable compiler/runtime ompBAM available R library

title: Bioconductor-Friendly Multithreaded BAM Processing
code: if (!requireNamespace("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE)) {   install.packages("BiocManager") } BiocManager::install("ompBAM")  if (!requireNamespace("remotes", quietly = TRUE)) {   install.packages("remotes") } remotes::install_github("cparsania/BamScale")

title: Bioconductor-Friendly Multithreaded BAM Processing
Bioconductor-Friendly Multithreaded BAM Processing
code: library(BamScale)  bam <- ompBAM::example_BAM("Unsorted")  # 1) Step1-style extraction x <- bam_read(   file = bam,   what = c("qname", "flag", "rname", "pos", "mapq", "cigar"),   threads = 4 )  # 2) Seq/qual in comparator-compatible mode sq <- bam_read(   file = bam,   what = c("qname", "seq", "qual"),   as = "data.frame",   seqqual_mode = "compatible",   threads = 4 )  # 2b) Seq/qual in compact mode (returns raw vectors, not plain strings) sq_compact <- bam_read(   file = bam,   what = c("qname", "qwidth", "seq", "qual"),   as = "data.frame",   seqqual_mode = "compact",   threads = 4 )  # 3) Fast chromosome-level counts cnt <- bam_count(file = bam, threads = 4)

title: Bioconductor-Friendly Multithreaded BAM Processing
text: Compact mode optimized BamScale-specific representation seq qual. return ordinary character strings: seq returned list-column raw vectors containing BAM-native packed sequence bytes (two bases per byte) qual returned list-column raw vectors containing per-base numeric Phred bytes qwidth required decode compact seq back base letters correctly quality byte value 255 represents missing quality Compact mode therefore best interpreted lower-level, deferred-decoding representation. useful extraction throughput matters immediate string materialization. downstream code expects ordinary sequence quality strings, use seqqual_mode = "compatible" instead, decode compact output explicitly:

title: Bioconductor-Friendly Multithreaded BAM Processing
text: BamScale can parallelize two axes: across files via BPPARAM workers, within file via OpenMP threads. Approximate effective concurrency: min(length(file), bpnworkers(BPPARAM)) * threads auto_threads = TRUE, BamScale preserves higher per-file thread counts possible reducing number concurrently active file workers shrinking per-file threads.

title: Bioconductor-Friendly Multithreaded BAM Processing
text: dedicated benchmark article summarizes current results relevant cross-tool comparisons: https://cparsania.github.io/BamScale/articles/benchmark-results.html vignettes/benchmark-results.Rmd article includes: step1, galignments, seqqual benchmark results fair cross-package comparisons Rsamtools GenomicAlignments compact-versus-compatible seqqual results BamScale

text: BamScale contributes performance claims, please also cite ompBAM.

title: Bioconductor-Friendly Multithreaded BAM Processing
text: Pull requests welcome. Please include: short motivation, tests behavior changes, benchmark evidence performance claims. opening PR, run:
code: R CMD build . R CMD check --as-cran BamScale_*.tar.gz Rscript -e "BiocCheck::BiocCheck('.')"

title: Bioconductor-Friendly Multithreaded BAM Processing
text: MIT (LICENSE).

what: BamScale: Bioconductor-Friendly Multithreaded BAM Processing — BamScale-package
title: BamScale: Bioconductor-Friendly Multithreaded BAM Processing — BamScale-package
text: Multithreaded sequential BAM processing built top ompBAM C++ engine. BamScale provides user-friendly BAM read scan interfaces designed compatibility existing Bioconductor workflows.

title: BamScale: Bioconductor-Friendly Multithreaded BAM Processing — BamScale-package
text: Maintainer: Chirag Parsania chirag.parsania@gmail.com

what: Count BAM records with Bioconductor-compatible filtering — bam_count
title: Count BAM records with Bioconductor-compatible filtering — bam_count
text: bam_count() provides fast chromosome-level count summary, honoring key filtering fields ScanBamParam (mapqFilter, flag, ).

title: Count BAM records with Bioconductor-compatible filtering — bam_count
code: bam_count(   file,   param = NULL,   threads = 1L,   BPPARAM = BiocParallel::bpparam(),   auto_threads = FALSE,   include_unmapped = TRUE )

title: Count BAM records with Bioconductor-compatible filtering — bam_count
text: file BAM input (character, BamFile, BamFileList). param Optional Rsamtools::ScanBamParam (compatible list). threads Requested number OpenMP threads. May capped auto_threads = TRUE. BPPARAM BiocParallel parameter multi-file operation. Defaults BiocParallel::bpparam(). Set NULL force serial file processing. auto_threads Logical; TRUE BPPARAM multiple workers, BamScale adaptively avoids oversubscription preserving higher per-file OpenMP thread counts possible reducing number concurrently active file workers shrinking per-file threads. include_unmapped Whether include extra * row unmapped records.

title: Count BAM records with Bioconductor-compatible filtering — bam_count
text: one file: data.frame columns seqname, seqlength, count. multiple files: named list data.frames.

title: Count BAM records with Bioconductor-compatible filtering — bam_count
text: Parallelism behavior matches bam_read(): BPPARAM distributes work across BAM files, threads controls OpenMP work within file. auto_threads = TRUE BPPARAM multiple workers, BamScale first limits number concurrently active workers preserve requested per-file thread count within detected core budget, caps per-file OpenMP threads single file still oversubscribe machine.

title: Count BAM records with Bioconductor-compatible filtering — bam_count

what: Fast BAM reading with Bioconductor-compatible arguments — bam_read
title: Fast BAM reading with Bioconductor-compatible arguments — bam_read
text: bam_read() multithreaded sequential BAM reader built top ompBAM. interface designed familiar users Rsamtools::scanBam(), GenomicAlignments::readGAlignments(), GenomicAlignments::readGAlignmentPairs().

title: Fast BAM reading with Bioconductor-compatible arguments — bam_read
code: bam_read(   file,   param = NULL,   what = NULL,   tag = NULL,   as = c("DataFrame", "data.frame", "GAlignments", "GAlignmentPairs", "scanBam"),   seqqual_mode = c("compatible", "compact"),   threads = 1L,   BPPARAM = BiocParallel::bpparam(),   auto_threads = FALSE,   use.names = FALSE,   with.which_label = FALSE,   include_unmapped = TRUE )

title: Fast BAM reading with Bioconductor-compatible arguments — bam_read
text: file BAM input. Supported values : single BAM path (character(1)) multiple BAM paths, Rsamtools::BamFile, Rsamtools::BamFileList. param Optional Rsamtools::ScanBamParam (compatible list lightweight use). following fields honored: mapqFilter, flag, , , tag. Character vector fields return, similar scanBam(=...). Supported fields qname, flag, rname, strand, pos, qwidth, mapq, cigar, mrnm, mpos, isize, seq, qual. tag Character vector 2-letter tag names extract. Output format: "DataFrame": returns S4Vectors::DataFrame (default), "data.frame": returns base data.frame, "GAlignments": returns GenomicAlignments::GAlignments, "GAlignmentPairs": returns GenomicAlignments::GAlignmentPairs, "scanBam": returns scanBam()-shaped list--lists. seqqual_mode Controls representation seq/qual fields requested: "compatible" (default): return character vectors matching scanBam-style expectations, "compact": return lower-level raw list-columns faster/lower-overhead extraction. compact mode, seq returned one raw vector per read containing BAM-native packed sequence bytes (two bases per byte), qual returned one raw vector per read containing per-base Phred bytes. plain character strings. qwidth needed decode compact seq back base letters, qual values 255 correspond missing quality values. mode currently supported = "data.frame" = "DataFrame". threads Requested number OpenMP threads used reading/decompression. May capped auto_threads = TRUE. BPPARAM BiocParallel parameter used file contains one BAM. Defaults BiocParallel::bpparam(). Set NULL force serial file processing. auto_threads Logical; TRUE BPPARAM multiple workers, BamScale adaptively avoids oversubscription preserving higher per-file OpenMP thread counts possible reducing number concurrently active file workers shrinking per-file threads. use.names Passed alignment object conversion. TRUE, read names (qname) used object names. .which_label Logical; TRUE param includes , extra which_label column returned. include_unmapped Logical; whether unmapped records retained (subject param$flag constraints).

title: Fast BAM reading with Bioconductor-compatible arguments — bam_read
text: file length 1: one object format specified . file length > 1 (BamFileList): named list outputs, one per BAM file.

title: Fast BAM reading with Bioconductor-compatible arguments — bam_read
text: bam_read() intentionally column-compatible common BAM fields used Bioconductor workflows can used fast drop-reader conversion downstream classes. Parallelism model: BPPARAM parallelizes across files (one file per BiocParallel worker). threads parallelizes within file via OpenMP. Effective total concurrency approximately min(length(file), BiocParallel::bpnworkers(BPPARAM)) * threads. auto_threads = TRUE BPPARAM multiple workers, BamScale first limits number concurrently active workers preserve requested per-file thread count within detected core budget, caps per-file OpenMP threads single file still oversubscribe machine. Compatibility notes: Region filtering via param$supported sequential filter (index-jump random access). Flag filtering uses ScanBamFlag semantics converting logical flag requirements required-set required-unset bit masks. Tag values returned character columns. Scalar tags scalar strings; B tags comma-separated vectors. seqqual_mode = "compact" optimized throughput-oriented benchmarking returns raw list-columns seq/qual, ordinary sequence quality strings. representation, seq contains BAM-packed nucleotide bytes qual contains raw Phred bytes. Compact output intended users want defer avoid full string-materialization costs; use decode_compact_seq(), decode_compact_qual(), decode_seqqual_compact() decode compact output back standard string form needed. "GAlignments" "GAlignmentPairs" output exclude unmapped records. = "scanBam" returns strict scan-like list--lists: without param$, returns one unnamed batch; param$, returns one batch per range label (including empty ranges), requested fields tag values $tag. output mode, seq qual returned Biostrings::DNAStringSet Biostrings::PhredQuality closer scanBam() compatibility.

title: Fast BAM reading with Bioconductor-compatible arguments — bam_read
code: bam <- ompBAM::example_BAM("Unsorted")  # Familiar scanBam-like field selection x <- bam_read(bam, what = c("qname", "flag", "rname", "pos", "cigar"))  # Include sequence + quality y <- bam_read(bam, what = c("qname", "seq", "qual"), threads = 2)  # scanBam-shaped output z <- bam_read(bam, what = c("qname", "flag"), tag = "NM", as = "scanBam")

what: Decode compact BamScale quality output — decode_compact_qual
title: Decode compact BamScale quality output — decode_compact_qual
text: Decodes qual values returned bam_read(..., seqqual_mode = "compact") back ASCII Phred-quality strings.

title: Decode compact BamScale quality output — decode_compact_qual
Decode compact BamScale quality output — decode_compact_qual
text: qual list (list-column) raw vectors produced compact BamScale quality extraction.

title: Decode compact BamScale quality output — decode_compact_qual
text: character vector containing decoded quality strings. Entries -missing quality bytes returned "*", matching BamScale's compatibility mode.

title: Decode compact BamScale quality output — decode_compact_qual

what: Decode compact BamScale sequence output — decode_compact_seq
title: Decode compact BamScale sequence output — decode_compact_seq
text: Decodes seq values returned bam_read(..., seqqual_mode = "compact") back ordinary character strings.

title: Decode compact BamScale sequence output — decode_compact_seq
Decode compact BamScale sequence output — decode_compact_seq
text: seq list (list-column) raw vectors produced compact BamScale sequence extraction. qwidth Integer vector read widths. required compact sequence bytes use BAM's 4-bit packed encoding (two bases per byte).

title: Decode compact BamScale sequence output — decode_compact_seq
text: character vector containing decoded sequence strings.

title: Decode compact BamScale sequence output — decode_compact_seq

what: Decode compact seq and qual columns in BamScale output — decode_seqqual_compact
title: Decode compact seq and qual columns in BamScale output — decode_seqqual_compact
text: Convenience wrapper converting compact bam_read() result back ordinary sequence quality strings.

title: Decode compact seq and qual columns in BamScale output — decode_seqqual_compact
Decode compact seq and qual columns in BamScale output — decode_seqqual_compact
text: x data.frame, S4Vectors::DataFrame, list-like object containing compact BamScale seq /qual columns. seq_col Name compact sequence column. qual_col Name compact quality column. qwidth_col Name read-width column used decode compact sequence bytes.

title: Decode compact seq and qual columns in BamScale output — decode_seqqual_compact
text: x compact seq /qual columns replaced decoded character vectors. input class preserved.

title: Decode compact seq and qual columns in BamScale output — decode_seqqual_compact
code: x <- data.frame(qwidth = 4L) x$seq <- I(list(as.raw(c(0x12, 0x48)))) x$qual <- I(list(as.raw(c(0L, 1L, 2L, 3L)))) decode_seqqual_compact(x) #>   qwidth  seq qual #> 1      4 ACGT !"#$
